source: All-In Podcast — Liquidity Summit: secondary markets eating the IPO
summary: Neurobotics (European AI-powered logistics robotics company based in Germany) is a private investment opportunity — quiet, well-funded, $100M revenue, operating outside the high-visibility Silicon Valley ecosystem.
summary_extended: Friedberg highlights Neurobotics, a German AI-powered logistics robotics company, as a compelling private investment opportunity flying under the radar of the US-centric AI hype cycle. The company reportedly has $100M in revenue, strong execution, and backing from large institutional investors, yet lacks the visibility of Silicon Valley peers. Friedberg's contrarian appeal is that the best opportunities may lie outside the high-profile US ecosystem, where valuations are more reasonable and competition for deal flow is lower. Because Neurobotics is a private German company, there is no US-listed equity to trade; the thesis is relevant only to investors with access to private markets or European venture/growth vehicles. No publicly traded US-listed beneficiary is unambiguously implied by this thesis.
detail: Friedberg names Neurobotics as a private company pick. It is described as an AI-powered logistics robotics company based in Germany, with large investors, $100M revenue, and strong execution. The appeal is that it is under the radar relative to US-centric AI robotics plays.
confidence_evidence: Named explicitly as a specific pick by Friedberg with revenue and investor detail, but no confirmation of ownership or deep diligence shared.
quote_fragment: Neurobotics in Europe AI powered logistics robotics
